meta.title: G22 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 4 Series G22 engine — B48, B58, B47, B57. Real UK costs and honest reliability data for the second-generation coupé. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 4 Series G22 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G22 engine typically costs £2,200–£8,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on engine — the B48 420i sits at £2,200–£4,000, while the B57 M440d can reach £8,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on engine and specialist location. The G22's strong retained values mean replacement is almost always viable.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 4 Series G22 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30 straight-six in the M440i is considered the most robust engine in the G22 range — BMW's B58 has a strong reliability reputation with few reported failures. The B48 four-cylinder petrol is also highly reliable, sharing many design improvements with the B58 [PROVISIONAL — pending dedicated Engine Page].

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 4 Series G22?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system leaks on the B48 and B58 petrol engines are the most common issues — investigate any coolant loss immediately. The B47 and B57 diesel engines can suffer from EGR cooler failure, DPF clogging, and AdBlue system issues. The 48V mild-hybrid system on MHEV-equipped variants can develop battery or starter-generator problems.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 4 Series G22 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, the G22 is generally reliable — it uses BMW's proven B-family engines which have an excellent reliability record compared to older BMW engines. The B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. The mild-hybrid systems add complexity but are increasingly proven.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 4 Series G22 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine is a hard avoid, but the diesel variants require careful consideration — emission-related components can be costly to repair. The 48V MHEV system adds complexity. The M440i is expensive to maintain and replace, though it's the most reliable six-cylinder. For most buyers, the 420i or 430i petrol is the sweet spot.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G22 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant loss is the key warning sign on B48 and B58 petrol engines — investigate any drop in coolant level immediately. On diesel variants, reduced performance or warning lights may indicate EGR cooler or DPF issues. On MHEV variants, warning lights or drivability issues may indicate 48V system failure.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 4 Series G22 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £15,000 G22, a £2,800–£5,000 B47 recon is 19–33% — well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold [BMW-VERIFIED]. Even on a lower-value example, replacement is viable. The G22's strong retained values mean engine replacement is almost always a repair decision.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 4 Series G22 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 straight-six can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing. The B48 is similarly durable, with 120,000+ miles achievable. The B47 and B57 diesel families are also durable, though emission system maintenance is important. The 48V battery typically has a lifespan of 5–8 years.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G22 and the F32 4 Series?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G22 (2020–present) is the second-generation 4 Series Coupé, built on BMW's CLAR platform with updated B-family engines and 48V mild-hybrid technology. The F32 (2013–2020) was the first generation, built on the F30 platform with older N-family engines. The G22 is larger and features more advanced engine technology.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 4 Series G22 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the engine block. Tell us your variant (420i, 430i, M440i, 420d, 430d, M440d) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 4 Series G22 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G22 Coupé — spanning B48 four-cylinder petrols, B58 straight-six petrols, B47 four-cylinder diesels, and B57 straight-six diesels, all with eight-speed automatic transmission.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: All ratings are [PROVISIONAL] pending dedicated Engine Pages. The B58 straight-six is widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern engine — the B58 has a strong reputation with few reported failures. The B48 four-cylinder is also robust, sharing many design improvements with the B58. The B47 and B57 diesel families are significantly more reliable than the older N47 they replaced, though emission-related components (EGR, DPF, AdBlue) can be problematic.

overview.intro: The G22 is the second generation of the BMW 4 Series Coupé, unveiled in June 2020 as the successor to the F32. It shares the CLAR platform with the G20 3 Series and features a longer wheelbase, wider track, and the controversial vertical kidney grille that defines this generation's design language. The engine line-up is all-turbocharged, featuring B48 four-cylinder petrols, B58 straight-six petrols (with 48V mild-hybrid assistance on the M440i), B47 four-cylinder diesels, and B57 straight-six diesels (both with mild-hybrid technology). A significant 2024 LCI (Life Cycle Impulse) facelift brought updated power outputs, mild-hybrid electrification to more variants, and the BMW Curved Display with Operating System 8.5.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR (Cluster Architecture) • Predecessor: F32 4 Series Coupé • Successor: None (current generation) • Sibling body styles in this generation: G23 Convertible, G26 Gran Coupé.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The G22 4 Series Coupé generated 120+ real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], concentrated on the 420i petrol and 420d diesel as the range reaches the used market.

items.quote: BMW's B58 straight-six is widely regarded as the most reliable modern BMW engine. Smooth, responsive, and with 48V mild-hybrid assistance for seamless stop-start. The M440i offers effortless performance with 374 bhp and 500 Nm of torque, making it the pinnacle of the G22 petrol range.
items.whoItsFor: enthusiasts who want the ultimate G22 experience.

items.quote: The most powerful diesel in the G22 range — 340 bhp and 700 Nm of torque from a 3.0-litre straight-six diesel with mild-hybrid assistance. Refined, effortless, and surprisingly efficient for its performance level.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel torque with six-cylinder refinement.

items.quote: The B47 is significantly more reliable than the N47 it replaced, but diesel emission components (EGR cooler, DPF, AdBlue) remain a potential cost. The mild-hybrid system adds complexity versus non-hybrid alternatives.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who prioritise fuel economy and are willing to maintain the diesel system carefully.

items.quote: The B58 is the most expensive engine to replace in the G22 range — a full reconditioned unit can exceed £7,000. Cooling system leaks and oil filter housing failures are the primary concerns.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want six-cylinder performance and have warranty cover.

items.quote: The entry-level petrol is the smart buy for most UK buyers — 184 bhp is more than adequate for daily driving, the B48 is proven and reliable, and running costs are significantly lower than the six-cylinder options. The 420i offers the best blend of performance, efficiency and value in the G22 range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want coupé style with sensible running costs.

items.quote: The 430i sits between the 420i and M440i — and it's the sweet spot for many buyers. With 245–258 bhp and 400 Nm of torque, it's quicker than the 420i (0-62 mph in 5.8 seconds vs 7.5) without the M440i's six-cylinder running costs. The post-LCI 430i with 258 bhp is particularly impressive.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want strong performance without the M440i's premium.

rows.commonMajorFailure: Coolant leaks (oil filter housing, vent pipe)

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Petrol — the most robust six-cylinder.

economicsRule.title: The G22 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,800–£5,000 B47 recon against a £15,000–£40,000 G22 sits at 7–33% of the car's value【EM-VERIFIED】. Even on a lower-value example, that's well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold — replacement is almost always viable. The G22's strong retained values mean engine replacement is a repair decision, not a financial one. For M440i cars (£25,000–£45,000+), even the most expensive replacement is comfortably viable.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G22 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: B48 420i — simple four-cylinder, proven reliability, lower running costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G22 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: B58 M440i — six-cylinder running costs and higher replacement cost.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G22 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: B48 430i — strong performance, proven B48 reliability, and the sweet spot of the range.

problems.title: B48 / B58 Cooling System Leaks (Oil Filter Housing)
problems.rootCause: The oil filter housing on B48 and B58 engines is a known weak point — the insert in one of the oil filter housing channels is made from a material that cannot withstand constant temperature changes, leading to coolant leaks. Coolant can leak from the oil filter housing and coolant vent pipe. If ignored, this can lead to overheating and engine damage.

tieredCosts.work: Oil filter housing replacement, coolant system flush
Full engine replacement if overheating caused damage

problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system inspected annually and investigate any coolant loss immediately — a £500–£1,000 oil filter housing replacement is dramatically cheaper than the £3,800+ engine replacement that follows if ignored. Check for coolant stains under the car or a sweet smell in the cabin.

cta.label: Compare B48/B58 engine prices for your G22 →

problems.title: B48 / B58 Water Pump Failure
problems.rootCause: The electric water pump on B48 and B58 engines can fail prematurely — this is a known weakness across BMW's B-family engines. Failure leads to sudden overheating and potential engine damage.

tieredCosts.work: Full engine replacement if overheating caused damage

problems.recommendation: Replace the water pump as preventative maintenance at 80,000 miles — a £400–£800 specialist job is trivial against the £3,800+ engine replacement that follows an ignored overheating event.

cta.label: Compare B48/B58 engine prices for your G22 →

problems.title: B47 / B57 Diesel EGR Cooler & Emission System Issues
problems.rootCause: The B47 and B57 diesel engines can suffer from EGR cooler failure — internal leaks allow coolant to enter the exhaust system. DPF clogging (urban driving) and AdBlue system issues are also common. BMW has issued recalls for EGR cooler issues on some models.

tieredCosts.work: EGR cooler replacement, DPF clean, AdBlue system service
Full engine replacement if coolant contamination caused damage

problems.recommendation: Check your diesel G22's VIN against any outstanding EGR cooler recalls — if affected, the repair is free. For DPF health, ensure regular motorway driving to allow regeneration cycles to complete. Have the emission system inspected at 60,000 miles.

cta.label: Compare B47/B57 diesel engine prices for your G22 →

problems.title: G22 48V Mild-Hybrid System Issues
problems.rootCause: The 48V mild-hybrid system adds complexity — the 48V battery, starter-generator, and associated electronics can develop issues. The 48V battery typically has a lifespan of 5–8 years.
problems.recommendation: Have the 48V system inspected during annual servicing — a £500–£1,000 check is cheap insurance against a £2,800+ system replacement. The mild-hybrid system improves efficiency and refinement — protect it.

replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the EM_Proprietary_Data baseline figures — G22 4 Series shares the CLAR platform with the G20 3 Series, with similar engine-bay access, so costs are inherited directly per the Authority Model, with no divergence note required.

M440i, 420d, 430d and M440d feature 48V mild-hybrid technology. 430i power output increased from 245 bhp to 258 bhp with the 2024 LCI facelift.

items.question: How much does a BMW 4 Series G22 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned G22 engine typically costs £2,200–£8,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on engine — the B48 420i sits at £2,200–£4,000, while the B57 M440d can reach £8,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on engine and specialist location. The G22's strong retained values mean replacement is almost always viable.

items.question: Which BMW 4 Series G22 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The B58B30 straight-six in the M440i is considered the most robust engine in the G22 range — BMW's B58 has a strong reliability reputation with few reported failures. The B48 four-cylinder petrol (420i/430i) is also highly reliable, sharing many design improvements with the B58 [PROVISIONAL — pending dedicated Engine Page].

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW 4 Series G22?
items.answer: Cooling system leaks (oil filter housing, water pump, vent pipe) on the B48 and B58 petrol engines are the most common issues — investigate any coolant loss immediately. The B47 and B57 diesel engines can suffer from EGR cooler failure, DPF clogging, and AdBlue system issues. The 48V mild-hybrid system on MHEV-equipped variants can develop battery or starter-generator problems.

items.question: Is the BMW 4 Series G22 reliable?
items.answer: Yes, the G22 is generally reliable — it uses BMW's proven B-family engines (B48, B58, B47, B57) which have an excellent reliability record compared to older BMW engines. The B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. The mild-hybrid systems add complexity but are increasingly proven. The G22's CLAR platform is shared with the G20 3 Series, which has a strong reliability reputation.

items.question: Which BMW 4 Series G22 engine should I avoid?
items.answer: No engine is a hard avoid, but the diesel variants (420d, 430d, M440d) require careful consideration — emission-related components (EGR, DPF, AdBlue) can be costly to repair. The 48V MHEV system adds complexity to any engine that has it (M440i, 420d, 430d, M440d). The M440i is expensive to maintain and replace, though it's the most reliable six-cylinder. For most buyers, the 420i or 430i petrol is the sweet spot.

items.question: How do I know if my G22 engine needs replacing?
items.answer: Coolant loss is the key warning sign on B48 and B58 petrol engines — investigate any drop in coolant level immediately. On diesel variants, reduced performance or warning lights may indicate EGR cooler or DPF issues. On MHEV variants, warning lights or drivability issues may indicate 48V system failure. Use our diagnostic tool for a personalised assessment.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW 4 Series G22 engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £15,000 G22, a £2,800–£5,000 B47 recon is 19–33% of the car's value — well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold [BMW-VERIFIED]. Even on a lower-value example, replacement is viable. The G22's strong retained values mean engine replacement is almost always a repair decision, not a financial one.

items.question: How long do BMW 4 Series G22 engines last?
items.answer: The B58 straight-six can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing — it's considered BMW's most robust modern engine. The B48 is similarly durable, with 120,000+ miles achievable. The B47 and B57 diesel families are also durable, though emission system maintenance is important. The 48V battery typically has a lifespan of 5–8 years before replacement may be needed.

items.question: What is the difference between the G22 and the F32 4 Series?
items.answer: The G22 (2020–present) is the second-generation 4 Series Coupé, built on BMW's CLAR platform with updated B-family engines and 48V mild-hybrid technology. The F32 (2013–2020) was the first generation, built on the F30 3 Series platform with the older N-family engines (N20, N55, N47). The G22 is larger, has a more controversial grille design, and features more advanced engine technology.
